threat_description: Ransomware deployment targeting core banking and on-premises infrastructure via hybrid AD compromise, with double extortion (data exfiltration + encryption)
source_detail: ENISA Ch.4 (prime threat #1); IOCTA Sec.2.1-2.2 (RaaS landscape, LockBit, Cl0p, 8Base, Akira)
relevance_to_org: Direct: T24 on-premises, hybrid AD with Azure AD Connect, partial air-gap backups, after-hours SOC gap. LockBit/Cl0p target financial sector (ENISA Fig.25).

threat_description: Ransomware targeting virtualisation infrastructure (ESXi, Azure VMs) to maximize disruption of migrated workloads
source_detail: ENISA Sec.2.2 (cybercrime actor trends: targeting virtualisation environments)
relevance_to_org: Direct: ~80 Azure VMs hosting migrated workloads; Windows Server 2019/2022 on-prem. ESXi variants (LockBit, BlackCat) documented.

threat_description: Exploitation of known vulnerabilities in perimeter devices (Palo Alto PAN-OS, Cisco AnyConnect) as ransomware initial access vector
source_detail: KEV: CVE-2024-0012/9474 (PAN-OS auth bypass + cmd injection, ransomware-associated); ENISA Sec.2.2 (zero-day/one-day exploits)
relevance_to_org: Direct: Palo Alto NGFW deployed at zone boundaries; Cisco AnyConnect VPN for 40% hybrid workforce.

threat_description: Spear-phishing targeting employees with credential harvesting pages mimicking M365 login or Azure portal, exploiting hybrid work VPN credential reset pretexts
source_detail: ENISA Ch.6 (phishing as #1 initial vector, 73% of social eng. breaches); IOCTA Sec.4.1 (phishing-as-a-service)
relevance_to_org: Direct: 40% hybrid staff using VPN. M365 environment. Financial sector targeted in 10% of social eng. events (ENISA Fig.7).

threat_description: Business Email Compromise (BEC) targeting treasury/CFO staff for fraudulent payment instructions or SWIFT-related pretexts
source_detail: ENISA Sec.6.1 (BEC: $4.67M avg breach cost, 22,000 FBI complaints); IOCTA Sec.4.3 (BEC/CEO fraud key EU threat)
relevance_to_org: Direct: SWIFT operations, treasury staff, M365 email. Financial sector highly targeted for BEC.

threat_description: Adversary-in-the-Middle (AiTM) phishing to bypass MFA and steal post-authentication session tokens for M365/Azure access
source_detail: ENISA Sec.6.6 (MFA bypass via AiTM proxies like Evilginx2, intercepting session tokens)
relevance_to_org: Direct: MFA enforced for remote access and cloud admin. AiTM bypasses MFA entirely by capturing session cookies.

threat_description: AI-enhanced social engineering using deepfakes and LLM-generated phishing content for more convincing targeted attacks
source_detail: ENISA Sec.6.8 (AI in social engineering); IOCTA Sec.5.1 (AI-assisted cybercrime, FraudGPT)
relevance_to_org: Relevant: Lowers barrier for targeted attacks against NordFinans staff. AI deepfakes for vishing.
verification_notes: Generic AI-enhanced social engineering trend; not org-specific; hard to map beyond T1566

threat_description: Call-back phishing combining email phishing with vishing (phone-based social engineering) to deliver malicious payloads
source_detail: ENISA Sec.6.7 (call-back phishing, Silent Ransom Group, fraudulent call centres)
relevance_to_org: Relevant: Bypasses email-only technical controls. Targets employees directly.
verification_notes: Somewhat generic call-back phishing; maps to T1566 but limited additional ATT&CK specificity

threat_description: Credential stuffing and account takeover against web/mobile banking portals using breach datasets and automated tooling
source_detail: IOCTA Sec.4.2 (ATO as growing threat); ENISA Sec.6.1 (credential theft)
relevance_to_org: Direct: ~200,000 active monthly web banking users. Mobile banking API. Credential stuffing low-risk/high-profit for criminals.

threat_description: Brute force and password spraying against hybrid AD and VPN endpoints, leveraging leaked credentials from third-party breaches
source_detail: ENISA Sec.2.1 (targeting identities); KEV: CVE-2020-1472 (Zerologon privilege escalation)
relevance_to_org: Direct: Hybrid AD with ~3,500 accounts. MFA not enforced for all internal apps.

threat_description: Information stealer malware (Redline, Vidar) targeting employee endpoints to harvest browser credentials, session tokens, and VPN passwords
source_detail: ENISA Sec.5.1 (info stealers persist and evolve, essential in attack chains); ENISA Sec.2.2 (rise of info stealers); IOCTA Sec.2.3 (Redline MaaS)
relevance_to_org: Direct: CrowdStrike Falcon EDR deployed but info stealers increasingly evasive. Harvested VPN/M365 credentials enable initial access.

threat_description: Malware delivery via novel mechanisms (OneNote, ISO, LNK files) bypassing legacy email security and Mark-of-the-Web protections
source_detail: ENISA Sec.5.2 (new malware delivery mechanisms)
relevance_to_org: Relevant: Microsoft Defender for Office 365 deployed. Novel delivery bypasses traditional attachment blocking.

threat_description: Mobile banking trojan targeting NordFinans mobile banking app users on Android via fake apps or overlay attacks
source_detail: ENISA Sec.5.3 (surge in mobile banking trojans, increasing complexity)
relevance_to_org: Direct: Native iOS/Android mobile banking apps. ~800 corporate-managed mobile devices.

threat_description: Malware-as-a-Service (MaaS) loaders/droppers (IcedID, Pikabot, SystemBC, DanaBot) used as precursors for ransomware or data theft
source_detail: ENISA Sec.5.4 (MaaS significant threat); IOCTA Sec.2.3 (QakBot alternatives)
relevance_to_org: Relevant: These loaders enable ransomware deployment. Financial sector specifically targeted by dropper campaigns.
verification_notes: Describes precursor MaaS tooling, not a specific threat scenario; may be too generic per GP5

threat_description: Compromise of third-party software supply chain (CI/CD pipelines, package repositories) injecting malicious code into production banking applications
source_detail: ENISA Sec.1.5 (XZ Utils backdoor, supply chain via social engineering); CISA KEV: CVE-2025-30066/30154 (GitHub Actions malicious code)
relevance_to_org: Direct: Azure DevOps CI/CD pipelines. .NET/Java/Python development. Internal NuGet/Maven/pip packages.

threat_description: Compromise of MSSP (Nordic managed security provider) used as pivot into NordFinans monitoring infrastructure and Sentinel configuration
source_detail: ENISA Sec.1.6 (ICT service management 3% of supply chain events); ENISA Sec.2.1 (cloud services for stealth)
relevance_to_org: Direct: Nordic MSSP provides after-hours SOC via VPN + API integration. MSSP compromise blinds detection.

threat_description: Compromise of Temenos T24 vendor support VPN to access core banking system directly
source_detail: ENISA Sec.1.5 (third-party supply chain attacks documented); ATT&CK T1199 Trusted Relationship
relevance_to_org: Direct: Temenos rated Critical. Vendor support VPN provides trusted access to T24 environment.

threat_description: Compromise of Euronet (ATM processor) via dedicated VPN to access ATM network segment and potentially pivot to internal banking network
source_detail: ENISA Sec.1.5 (supply chain); IOCTA Sec.4.5 (ATM attacks decreased but still present)
relevance_to_org: Direct: ~120 ATMs managed by Euronet via dedicated VPN tunnels. Man-in-the-middle and relay attacks noted.

threat_description: Dependency confusion or typosquatting attack targeting internal package namespaces in Azure DevOps build pipelines
source_detail: ENISA Sec.1.5 (name confusion, tricking developers); KEV supply chain entries
relevance_to_org: Direct: Azure DevOps with .NET/Java/Python packages. Internal package names potentially discoverable via job postings.

threat_description: Exfiltration of customer PII and financial data from cloud storage (Azure Blob) or M365 SharePoint via misconfigured access controls during cloud migration
source_detail: ENISA Ch.7 (threats against data); ENISA Sec.7.5 (cloud computing and data breaches)
relevance_to_org: Direct: Cloud migration in progress. Azure workload security controls still being standardized. ~500,000 customer records.

threat_description: Data exfiltration from core banking Oracle DB via SQL injection or direct database access following lateral movement
source_detail: ENISA Ch.7 (data breaches); KEV: Oracle E-Business Suite entries (CVE-2025-61884 SSRF)
relevance_to_org: Direct: Temenos T24 on Oracle DB. Legacy Java customizations increase attack surface.

threat_description: Insider-driven data exfiltration of customer financial records or SWIFT transaction data by privileged employee
source_detail: ENISA Sec.7.1 (data breach trends); IOCTA Sec.4.2 (ATO and data theft)
relevance_to_org: Direct: CyberArk manages ~200 privileged accounts. Quarterly recertification may miss role changes. SWIFT operator access.

threat_description: Volumetric DDoS attack against Azure Front Door endpoints (web/mobile banking) causing service disruption
source_detail: ENISA Ch.8 (DDoS prime threat #2; banking 12% of DDoS targets); ENISA Sec.8.1 (attacks larger, more complex, cheaper)
relevance_to_org: Direct: Web banking behind Azure Front Door WAF+CDN. Banking sector is 3rd most DDoS-targeted (ENISA).

threat_description: Application-layer (L7) DDoS targeting API gateway and authentication endpoints with crafted requests that bypass WAF rules
source_detail: ENISA Sec.8.7 (application vs network attacks); ENISA Sec.8.5 (DDoS as smokescreen)
relevance_to_org: Direct: PSD2 APIs via Azure API Management. REST API endpoints potentially less rate-limited than web portal.

threat_description: Ransom DDoS (RDoS) with extortion demand threatening sustained attacks unless payment is made
source_detail: ENISA Sec.8.6 (Ransom Denial of Service)
relevance_to_org: Relevant: Financial institutions are prime RDoS targets due to availability requirements.
verification_notes: RDoS overlaps with T-023/T-024 (DDoS); may be redundant

threat_description: DDoS as distraction/smokescreen for simultaneous intrusion activity (initial access, privilege escalation)
source_detail: ENISA Sec.8.5 (DDoS as smokescreen and horizontal attacks)
relevance_to_org: Direct: Small SOC team of 5 analysts. DDoS incident would consume SOC attention, enabling parallel intrusion.

threat_description: Exploitation of known vulnerabilities in Microsoft Windows (privilege escalation, CLFS driver, kernel) to achieve SYSTEM-level access on endpoints
source_detail: KEV: CVE-2025-29824 (CLFS use-after-free, ransomware); CVE-2024-49039 (Task Scheduler); CVE-2024-30088 (kernel TOCTOU)
relevance_to_org: Direct: ~2,200 Windows 11 endpoints. Windows Server 2019/2022 on-premises.

threat_description: Exploitation of Microsoft SharePoint/Exchange vulnerabilities for remote code execution on collaboration platforms
source_detail: KEV: CVE-2025-53770, CVE-2025-49704 (SharePoint RCE, ransomware); CVE-2021-26855 (ProxyLogon)
relevance_to_org: Direct: SharePoint Online via M365. Exchange Online. Historical ProxyLogon attacks targeted financial sector.

threat_description: Exploitation of Log4j (CVE-2021-44228) or Java deserialization vulnerabilities in Temenos T24 legacy Java components
source_detail: KEV: CVE-2021-44228 (Log4Shell, ransomware); ENISA Sec.3.4 (KEV analysis)
relevance_to_org: Direct: T24 has legacy Java customizations on older versions. Oracle DB backend. Java ecosystem widely targeted.

threat_description: Exploitation of SAP NetWeaver vulnerabilities to compromise S/4HANA Cloud ERP/HR system
source_detail: KEV: CVE-2025-31324 (file upload, ransomware); CVE-2020-6287 (missing auth)
relevance_to_org: Direct: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for financials and HR. Contains employee PII and financial data.

threat_description: SWIFT messaging fraud via compromised operator credentials or lateral movement into SWIFT secure zone, targeting Alliance Lite2 for fraudulent MT103/MT202 messages
source_detail: ATT&CK: Lazarus Group (G0032), APT38 (G0082) — documented SWIFT-targeting campaigns (Bangladesh Bank, FASTCash)
relevance_to_org: Direct: Alliance Lite2 for interbank messaging. CyberArk-vaulted SWIFT operator credentials. After-hours SOC gap.

threat_description: Targeting of SWIFT secure zone HSMs or key management infrastructure to forge or manipulate transaction authentication
source_detail: SWIFT CSP threat intelligence: attacks on HSM-protected key material documented in SWIFT ISAC reports
relevance_to_org: Direct: SWIFT secure zone with HSMs. Compromise of HSM keys would enable undetected fraudulent transactions.
verification_notes: Source (SWIFT CSP) not directly consulted; content inferred, not extracted from document

threat_description: Malicious or compromised Third-Party Provider (TPP) abusing PSD2 Open Banking APIs for unauthorized account enumeration or payment initiation
source_detail: IOCTA Sec.4.6 (online fraud and money laundering); PSD2 regulatory context
relevance_to_org: Direct: PSD2-compliant APIs exposed via Azure API Management with OAuth 2.0. ~500,000 retail customers accessible via AISP scope.

threat_description: Digital skimming attacks targeting web banking portal payment pages to intercept customer card data or transaction details
source_detail: IOCTA Sec.4.4 (digital skimming persistent threat; e-merchants and banks most targeted)
relevance_to_org: Direct: .NET web banking portal on Azure App Service. ~200,000 active monthly users.

threat_description: ATM jackpotting or black-box attacks via physical access to ATMs or network-level manipulation through Euronet VPN
source_detail: IOCTA Sec.4.5 (ATM attacks: man-in-the-middle and relay attacks continue despite 40% decrease)
relevance_to_org: Direct: ~120 ATMs across Sweden managed by Euronet. Dedicated VPN tunnels.

threat_description: Nation-state cyber espionage targeting financial intelligence, correspondent banking relationships, and M&A data
source_detail: ENISA Sec.2.1 (state-nexus group trends); ATT&CK: Lazarus (G0032), APT38 (G0082) — financial sector targeting
relevance_to_org: Relevant: Publicly traded bank on Nasdaq Stockholm. SWIFT correspondent relationships. Strategic intelligence value.

threat_description: APT lateral movement using Living-off-the-Land techniques (LOTL) to evade CrowdStrike EDR and blend into normal administrative activity
source_detail: ENISA Sec.2.2 (LOTL for evasion); ATT&CK: FIN7 (G0046), TA505 (G0092) use signed binaries, PowerShell
relevance_to_org: Direct: CrowdStrike Falcon EDR deployed. LOTL techniques use legitimate system tools (PSExec, WMI, PowerShell).

threat_description: APT leveraging cloud services (Azure, M365) for C2 communications to blend with legitimate organizational cloud traffic
source_detail: ENISA Sec.2.1 (leveraging cloud services for stealth and strategy, disguising C2 as ordinary traffic)
relevance_to_org: Direct: Heavy Azure and M365 usage. Cloud-based C2 traffic would blend with legitimate Azure traffic from NordFinans.

threat_description: Azure cloud misconfiguration exploitation during active migration — exposed storage accounts, management APIs, or overly permissive Managed Identity roles
source_detail: ENISA Sec.7.5 (cloud computing and data breaches); ENISA Sec.2.2 (relying on cloud services)
relevance_to_org: Direct: Cloud migration in progress. Security controls for Azure workloads still being standardized (acknowledged gap).

threat_description: Abuse of Azure AD Connect synchronization account to pivot from compromised on-premises AD into Azure tenant (or vice versa)
source_detail: ENISA Sec.2.1 (targeting identities); KEV: CVE-2020-1472 (Zerologon enables domain compromise)
relevance_to_org: Direct: Hybrid AD with Azure AD Connect. Sync account has high privileges in both environments.

threat_description: Exploitation of after-hours SOC coverage gap (18:00-08:00 CET) for extended dwell-time attacks with degraded detection
source_detail: ENISA Sec.2.2 (cybercrime actor trends: timing attacks); confirmed org gap in profile
relevance_to_org: Direct: Internal SOC business-hours only. MSSP monitoring has less institutional context and higher escalation latency.

threat_description: Abuse of M365 OAuth consent grants to establish persistent access to mailboxes and SharePoint, surviving credential resets
source_detail: ENISA Sec.2.1 (state-nexus actors targeting identities); ENISA Sec.2.2 (relying on cloud services)
relevance_to_org: Direct: M365 environment. OAuth consent grants are persistent and often not reviewed.

threat_description: Kerberoasting or AS-REP roasting against hybrid AD service accounts to obtain crackable ticket hashes for privilege escalation
source_detail: ATT&CK: FIN7 (G0046) uses Kerberoasting; Carbanak (G0008) targets AD; TA505 (G0092) credential theft
relevance_to_org: Direct: Hybrid AD with ~3,500 accounts including service accounts. MFA not enforced for internal apps.

threat_description: Hacktivist DDoS and defacement campaigns motivated by geopolitical events targeting Nordic financial institutions
source_detail: ENISA Sec.2.4 (hacktivists: conflict-driven, DDoS and defacements, potential state-nexus links)
relevance_to_org: Relevant: Swedish bank in Nordic region. Geopolitical tensions drive hacktivist targeting of financial sector.
verification_notes: Generic hacktivist DDoS; overlaps with T-023; relevance is Relevant not Direct

threat_description: Money mule networks and cryptocurrency laundering leveraging compromised customer accounts for layering stolen funds
source_detail: IOCTA Sec.4.6 (online fraud and money laundering); IOCTA Sec.1.1 (cryptocurrency criminal use)
relevance_to_org: Relevant: ~500,000 retail customers. Compromised accounts can be used for money mule operations.
verification_notes: Downstream fraud consequence (money mules); not a cyber threat with clear ATT&CK technique mapping
